title: Read Hermes profiles implementation
completion_summary: Read hermes_cli/profiles.py + website/docs/user-guide/profiles.md. Documented: (1) isolation model — profile = independent HERMES_HOME, ~119 files route through get_hermes_home(), `default` profile = bare ~/.hermes; (2) explicit non-features — profiles are NOT a sandbox and NOT a workspace; (3) inheritance — none at runtime, only at creation time via three modes (blank, --clone copies _CLONE_CONFIG_FILES + _CLONE_SUBDIR_FILES + skills/, --clone-all full copytree then strips _CLONE_ALL_STRIP, recursion guard excludes profiles/ from source root); (4) three activation paths (wrapper script in ~/.local/bin, -p flag, sticky active_profile file with atomic write); (5) name validation (regex + _RESERVED_NAMES + _HERMES_SUBCOMMANDS); (6) directory bootstrap including per-profile home/ for subprocess credentials; (7) seed_profile_skills via subprocess to avoid HERMES_HOME caching; (8) export exclusions for default profile; (9) Apprentice implications — run trainer in its own profile, fork for eval via --clone-all, profiles don't sandbox so Firecracker still does that job. Deliverable: notes/profiles-implementation.md.
